preamble: The transformation from a voiced palatal lateral approximant (/ʎ/) to a palatalized voiced alveolar lateral approximant (/lʲ/) represents a shift in the primary place of articulation. This involves the tongue contact moving from the hard palate to the alveolar ridge, while a secondary palatalization, characterized by the raising of the tongue body towards the palate, is maintained. This process can be understood as a form of depalatalization of the primary articulation.
phoneticEffects: Depalatalization of primary articulation (from palatal to alveolar), with retention of secondary palatalization

examples.note: In Brazilian Portuguese, the voiced palatal lateral approximant [ʎ] exhibits allophonic variation with a palatalized alveolar lateral [lʲ], with articulatory studies suggesting they may function as variants of the same category.
The Proto-Slavic palatal variant *ľ was realized as [ʎ ~ lʲ]. In East and West Slavic branches, depalatalization or other shifts occurred, indicating a tendency towards or realization as [lʲ].
